meta.subdomain: unexplained nighttime weight loss in beehives at a research apiary

visible.story: The Larkmeadow research apiary sits on a low rise above a clover pasture, with eight instrumented Langstroth hives arranged on a single stand. Each hive has a load cell, an internal temperature and humidity probe, and an entrance counter. Lead technician Pri has been puzzled for two weeks: hive masses drop noticeably overnight, more than evaporation or normal respiration would predict, and morning entrance boards show small piles of wing fragments and dead workers. The colonies themselves seem queenright and brood patterns look normal. Pri notes a few contextual details in her logbook. The nectar flow has been weak since the clover finished, so foragers come home light. A commercial outfit set up roughly two hundred strong production colonies in a hedgerow about four hundred meters east last month, and their bees can be seen working the same pasture during the day. A skunk was photographed on the trailcam twice. The entrance reducers on three hives have warped and no longer seat tightly. Ambient nights have been warm and still. Varroa boards show low mite drop. Pri wants to know whether to invest in mite treatment, predator fencing, feeding, ventilation changes, or something else before she loses winter stores.
visible.question: Do not give a black-box prediction. Use the available records and targeted tests to explain what hidden story-plausible cause best accounts for the outcome pattern. Your answer should name the unobserved cause in ordinary language, give evidence from queried data, rule out plausible alternatives, state a decisive test, and recommend the next action (including its dose/level where applicable). The action names are intentionally neutral and do NOT reveal which one targets the cause; decide that from the data you collect, not from the labels.

observed_variables.name: NeighborYardForagerDensity
observed_variables.description: midday count index of non-resident bees observed working the shared pasture

scale.higher: more foreign foragers in the shared forage area

observed_variables.name: EntranceDebrisIndex
observed_variables.description: morning index of wing fragments and dead workers on the landing board

scale.higher: more fight debris at the entrance

observed_variables.name: NectarFlowStrength
observed_variables.description: estimated regional nectar availability from sentinel scale hive trend

observed_variables.name: EntranceReducerFitScore
observed_variables.description: tightness and integrity of installed entrance reducers across the stand

observed_variables.name: NightEntranceTrafficCount
observed_variables.description: infrared beam-break counts at the entrance between 21:00 and 04:00

observed_variables.name: GuardEngagementAcoustic
observed_variables.description: acoustic index of defensive piping and fight buzz inside the hive

observed_variables.name: EastSideStandPositionEffect
observed_variables.description: differential effect for hives positioned on the east end of the stand, nearest the hedgerow

observed_variables.name: MorningDeadBeeCount
observed_variables.description: count index of dead workers swept from landing board at dawn

scale.higher: more morning mortality at the entrance

observed_variables.name: OvernightMassLoss
observed_variables.description: drop in load cell reading from dusk to dawn corrected for humidity

observed_variables.name: VarroaMiteDropIndex
observed_variables.description: natural mite fall on sticky boards over 24 hours

observed_variables.name: MammalianPredatorActivity
observed_variables.description: trailcam-derived index of skunk and raccoon visits near the stand

scale.higher: more nocturnal mammal activity at hive fronts

observed_variables.name: InHiveHumidityIndex
observed_variables.description: average overnight relative humidity inside the brood box

scale.higher: more humid interior, suggesting ventilation problems

observed_variables.name: MarkedForeignForagerReturnRate
observed_variables.description: share of paint-marked foragers from the neighbor yard later detected at Larkmeadow entrances

scale.higher: more confirmed foreign bees entering study hives

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenA
intervenable_variables.description: graded reduction of entrance opening size across all hives using sized cleats

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenB
intervenable_variables.description: graded deployment of robbing screens with offset rerouted entrances

intervenable_variables.name: ProtocolX
intervenable_variables.description: paint-mark a sample of foragers at the neighboring commercial yard and monitor returns at Larkmeadow

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenC
intervenable_variables.description: graded oxalic acid vapor miticide treatment schedule

intervenable_variables.name: SupplementalFeedSetpoint
intervenable_variables.description: syrup feeder fill level setpoint to offset stores loss

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenD
intervenable_variables.description: graded relocation of hives along the stand away from the east-facing hedgerow line

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenE
intervenable_variables.description: graded installation of electric predator fencing and skunk mats

intervenable_variables.name: ProtocolY
intervenable_variables.description: queen status and brood pattern inspection across all colonies

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenF
intervenable_variables.description: graded increase in upper ventilation via shimmed inner covers

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenG
intervenable_variables.description: graded repainting and external cleaning of hive boxes

public_action_space.note: Each interventional query may set any subset of public actions up to the cap. Actions are non-binary: most take an ordered dose level and some take a continuous setpoint, so choosing the right level is part of the task. Action names are neutral and do not indicate which knob addresses the hidden cause.

discovery_protocol.task_goal: Infer a story-plausible hidden cause, not just an observed predictor.
discovery_protocol.important_warning: Observed measurements may be clues, downstream symptoms, or competing alternatives; none of them is automatically the hidden cause. Action names are neutral and do not tell you which knob targets the cause, and the best level is not always the maximum.

template.subdomain: unexplained nighttime weight loss in beehives at a research apiary

hidden_cause.name: RobbingPressureFromNeighborColonies
hidden_cause.plain_name: nighttime robbing by bees from a strong neighboring apiary

template.story: The Larkmeadow research apiary sits on a low rise above a clover pasture, with eight instrumented Langstroth hives arranged on a single stand. Each hive has a load cell, an internal temperature and humidity probe, and an entrance counter. Lead technician Pri has been puzzled for two weeks: hive masses drop noticeably overnight, more than evaporation or normal respiration would predict, and morning entrance boards show small piles of wing fragments and dead workers. The colonies themselves seem queenright and brood patterns look normal. Pri notes a few contextual details in her logbook. The nectar flow has been weak since the clover finished, so foragers come home light. A commercial outfit set up roughly two hundred strong production colonies in a hedgerow about four hundred meters east last month, and their bees can be seen working the same pasture during the day. A skunk was photographed on the trailcam twice. The entrance reducers on three hives have warped and no longer seat tightly. Ambient nights have been warm and still. Varroa boards show low mite drop. Pri wants to know whether to invest in mite treatment, predator fencing, feeding, ventilation changes, or something else before she loses winter stores.

actions.name: RegimenA
actions.description: graded reduction of entrance opening size across all hives using sized cleats

actions.name: RegimenB
actions.description: graded deployment of robbing screens with offset rerouted entrances

actions.name: ProtocolX
actions.description: paint-mark a sample of foragers at the neighboring commercial yard and monitor returns at Larkmeadow

actions.name: RegimenC
actions.description: graded oxalic acid vapor miticide treatment schedule

actions.name: SupplementalFeedSetpoint
actions.description: syrup feeder fill level setpoint to offset stores loss

actions.name: RegimenD
actions.description: graded relocation of hives along the stand away from the east-facing hedgerow line

actions.name: RegimenE
actions.description: graded installation of electric predator fencing and skunk mats

actions.name: ProtocolY
actions.description: queen status and brood pattern inspection across all colonies

actions.name: RegimenF
actions.description: graded increase in upper ventilation via shimmed inner covers

actions.name: RegimenG
actions.description: graded repainting and external cleaning of hive boxes

gold_explanation.description: Strong colonies from the nearby commercial yard are robbing the study hives during a nectar dearth, removing stores overnight and producing fight debris at the entrances.
evidence: Overnight mass loss tracks neighbor-yard forager density and inversely tracks nectar flow, with the worst losses on east-side hives nearest the hedgerow.
Nighttime entrance traffic and defensive acoustic activity are elevated on high-loss nights, accompanied by wing-fragment debris and morning dead bees at the landing board.
Hives whose entrance reducers had warped (lower fit score) lose disproportionately more mass, consistent with undefendable entrances under robbing pressure.
alternatives_ruled_out: Varroa mite drop is low and uncorrelated with overnight mass loss, so parasite-driven decline does not fit.
Mammalian predator activity on the trailcam is sporadic and does not co-vary with mass loss; debris pattern is bee-on-bee fight residue, not mammal disturbance.
In-hive humidity is normal and stable, so evaporative or ventilation-driven mass loss cannot explain the magnitude observed.
gold_explanation.decisive_test: Paint-mark a sample of foragers at the neighboring commercial yard and verify that a substantial fraction of them are detected entering Larkmeadow hives on high-loss nights.
gold_explanation.why_action: Tightening entrances and deploying robbing screens (RegimenA and RegimenB at standard dose) directly blocks the inter-apiary robbing mechanism, whereas miticides, predator fencing, ventilation, or feeding only address symptoms or unrelated causes.

targeted_fix_primary.name: RegimenA
targeted_fix_primary.description: graded reduction of entrance opening size across all hives using sized cleats

targeted_fix_secondary.name: RegimenB
targeted_fix_secondary.description: graded deployment of robbing screens with offset rerouted entrances

weak_buffer.description: graded increase in upper ventilation via shimmed inner covers

cosmetic_action.name: RegimenG
cosmetic_action.description: graded repainting and external cleaning of hive boxes
